# Bynk

> Bynk is a statically typed, architecture-first programming language for building
> services. The shape of a program — its contexts, services, agents, and the types
> that flow between them — is part of the language, not a convention layered on top.
> Bynk compiles to typed TypeScript and targets Cloudflare Workers. It is pre-1.0
> and evolves in small, spec-first increments (current toolchain version 0.107).

This file is a self-contained primer for an LLM agent. The first half teaches
enough Bynk to read and write it; the second half links to the canonical [Bynk
Book](docs/) for depth. When in doubt, the Book documents *what compiles today*;
features marked "planned" are deferred, not available.

## Core ideas

- **Make illegal states unrepresentable.** Refined types (a base type carrying a
  predicate), opaque types, and errors-as-values (`Result`, `Option`) keep whole
  classes of bug unexpressed.
- **Architecture is in the language.** `context`, `service`, and stateful `agent`
  are first-class. A `context` is the unit of deployment — it compiles to one
  Cloudflare Worker.
- **Honest effects and capabilities.** Side effects have type `Effect[T]`.
  Dependencies are declared in signatures with `given`, supplied by the platform,
  and mockable in tests.
- **Compiles to readable, typed TypeScript.** The router, boundary validation, and
  Worker entry point are generated for you.
- **Testing is built in.** `test` blocks, `assert`, capability `mocks`, and
  `test integration` across Workers ship with the language.

## File and project shape

- Source files use the `.bynk` extension and begin with `context <name>` (or
  `commons <name>` for a pure, stateless module). Dotted names like
  `context hello.web` are allowed.
- A project has a `bynk.toml` manifest and a `src/` directory. `bynk new <name>`
  scaffolds one; `bynk dev` compiles and serves it locally on `http://localhost:8787`.
- `---` … `---` is a doc comment (markdown, attached to the following declaration).
  `--` begins a line comment.
- `uses <commons>` brings a commons module's types/functions into scope.
- `consumes bynk { Clock, Logger, Fetch }` imports first-party platform
  capabilities; `consumes bynk.cloudflare { Kv }` pins the context to Cloudflare.
- `consumes <Context> as <Alias>` depends on another context's exported services.

## Language tour (by example)

A minimal service:

```bynk
context greet

service api from http {
  on GET("/ping") by Visitor () -> Effect[HttpResult[String]] {
    Ok("pong")
  }
}
```

### Types

```bynk
type UserId = String where NonEmpty                  -- refined: base type + predicate
type Title  = String where NonEmpty and MaxLength(200)
type Qty    = Int where Positive

type Order = { id: String, customer: String }        -- record
type TodoError = enum { NotFound }                    -- payloadless sum type
opaque type Token = String                            -- opaque: structure hidden outside its module
```

- A refined type's constructor is `T.of(value) -> Result[T, ValidationError]`. At
  a service boundary, route params and request bodies are validated through `.of`
  automatically (HTTP 400 on failure).
- Records are constructed `Order { id: id, customer: c }` and updated functionally
  `TodoItem { ...it, done: true }`.
- Base types: `String`, `Int`, `Float`, `Bool`, `Duration` (ms, e.g. `5.minutes`),
  `Instant` (epoch ms, `Clock.now()`). Generic library types: `Option[T]`,
  `Result[T, E]`, `Effect[T]`, `List[T]`.

### Errors and optionals as values

```bynk
match Title.of(name) {
  Ok(title) => Ok(greeting(title))
  Err(_)    => BadRequest("name must be non-empty and at most 200 characters")
}

match found {                 -- found : Option[Item]
  Some(it) => Ok(it)
  None     => NotFound
}
```

- `Result[T, E]` has variants `Ok(T)` / `Err(E)`. `Option[T]` has `Some(T)` / `None`.
- `match` is exhaustive. `is` tests a value against a variant pattern, yielding a
  `Bool` (e.g. `if (mo is None) { 1 } else { 0 }`).
- `Json.decode[T](s) -> Result[T, JsonError]` and `Json.encode(v) -> String`.

### Effects and capabilities

Effectful work has type `Effect[T]`. Inside a handler, `let x <- expr` sequences
an effect (binds its result); `let x = expr` binds a pure value. `Effect.pure(v)`
lifts a value. A handler declares the capabilities it needs with `given`:

```bynk
capability Logger {                  -- a capability is a set of signatures, no bodies
  fn info(message: String) -> Effect[()]
}

on GET("/") by Visitor () -> Effect[HttpResult[String]] given Logger {
  let _ <- Logger.info("greeting the world")
  Ok("hello")
}
```

Capabilities are *provided* by the platform or a `provides` block, and replaced by
`mocks` in tests. `"\(expr)"` is string interpolation.

### Agents (stateful, keyed)

An `agent` is a keyed, stateful unit — one instance per key, compiling to a
Cloudflare Durable Object. State lives in `store` fields; `on call` handlers are
its methods, invoked as `AgentName(key).method(args)`.

```bynk
agent Todos {
  key owner: UserId                       -- the address; one instance per distinct key

  store items:   Map[String, TodoItem]    -- storage-backed collections
  store lastSeq: Cell[Int]                -- a single mutable cell

  on call add(title: Title) -> Effect[TodoItem] {
    let next = lastSeq + 1
    let item = TodoItem { id: "\(next)", seq: next, title: title, done: false }
    let _ <- items.put(item.id, item)
    lastSeq := next                        -- `:=` writes a store field
    item
  }

  on call pending() -> Effect[List[TodoItem]] {
    items.filter((it) => it.done == false).sortBy((it) => it.seq).collect()
  }
}
```

- Storage kinds: `Map[K, V]`, `Cell[T]`, `Cache[K, V]` (TTL-expiring, via
  `@ttl(30.minutes)`). `Map` fields can be `@indexed(by: field)` for indexed
  equality filters.
- Query combinators over storage: `filter`, `sortBy`, `groupBy`, `joinOn`,
  `leftJoin`, then a terminal `collect()` / `count()` / `sum(...)`.
- `invariant` declares a predicate that must hold of every committed state
  (runtime-checked); `implies` is logical implication in invariant predicates.
- `self` refers to the current instance inside a handler.

### Entry points (service protocols)

A `service X from <protocol>` groups handlers. Protocols are a closed set:

```bynk
service api from http {
  on POST("/todos") by u: User (body: AddRequest) -> Effect[HttpResult[TodoItem]] {
    let item <- Todos(u.identity).add(body.title)
    Created(item)
  }
}

service checks from cron {
  on schedule("*/5 * * * *") (at: Int) -> Effect[Result[(), String]] given Fetch { … }
}

service worker from queue("name") {
  on message (msg: Job) -> Effect[Result[(), String]] { … }
}

service chat from ws(in: ClientFrame, out: ServerFrame) {
  on open    by user: Participant (roomId: RoomId) -> Effect[()] { … }   -- exactly one; must transfer the connection to an agent
  on message by user: Participant (roomId: RoomId, frame: ClientFrame) -> Effect[()] { … }
  on close   by user: Participant (roomId: RoomId) -> Effect[()] { … }
}
```

- HTTP result constructors map to status codes: `Ok` (200), `Created` (201),
  `NoContent` (204), `BadRequest`, `NotFound`, `ServerError`, etc. The handler
  return type is `Effect[HttpResult[T]]`.
- Cron has no ambient clock — the scheduled instant `at` is passed in.

### Actors and access control

A handler names the actor it serves with `by`. `Visitor` is the built-in
anonymous public actor; a declared `actor` is an authenticated boundary contract:

```bynk
actor User { auth = Bearer(secret = "AUTH_JWT_SECRET"), identity = UserId }

on GET("/todos") by u: User () -> Effect[HttpResult[List[TodoItem]]] {
  let items <- Todos(u.identity).all()    -- u.identity is the verified, sealed UserId
  Ok(items)
}
```

The verified identity is *sealed* — minted at the boundary, never forged
downstream — and is commonly used as an agent key so each caller gets private state.

### Testing

```bynk
test "an over-limit request is denied" {
  let d = decide(0, 10, 5000, 60000, 10)
  assert d.allowed == false
  assert d.count == 10
}
```

- `test "<name>" { … }` is a case; `assert <bool-expr>` checks it.
- `mocks` supplies a fake capability implementation; `test integration` with
  `wires` stands up several contexts as Workers to test a cross-Worker flow.

## Keyword reference (59 reserved words)

Declarations: `context` `commons` `service` `agent` `actor` `capability`
`adapter` `type` `enum` `opaque` `record` `fn` `test` `invariant`.
Members/clauses: `on` `from` `by` `key` `store` `given` `provides` `consumes`
`exports` `transparent` `uses` `as` `wires` `mocks` `binding` `protocol`.
Expressions/control: `let` `match` `if` `else` `is` `self` `assert` `and`
`implies` `where`.
Protocols/built-ins: `http` `cron` `queue`. Literals: `true` `false`.
Types/variants: `Bool` `Int` `Float` `String` `Duration` `Instant` `Effect`
`Option` `Some` `None` `Result` `Ok` `Err` `ValidationError` `JsonError`.

## CLI

- `bynkc compile <src>` — compile to TypeScript. Also `bynkc check`, `bynkc fmt`,
  `bynkc test`.
- `bynk new <name>` — scaffold a project (no toolchain needed, writes files only).
- `bynk dev` — compile and serve locally. `bynk doctor` — check the environment.
- `bynkc-lsp` — the language server (diagnostics, hover, go-to-definition).

## Documentation (canonical, in this repo under docs/src/)

### Start here
- [What is Bynk?](docs/src/introduction/what-is-bynk.md): the language in brief.
- [Anatomy of a Bynk service](docs/src/introduction/anatomy-of-a-service.md): a full service, part by part.
- [Coming from TypeScript](docs/src/introduction/coming-from-typescript.md): the mental-model deltas.
- [Install](docs/src/introduction/install.md): build from source with a recent Rust toolchain.

### Tutorials (learn by building)
- [1. Compile your first program](docs/src/tutorials/01-first-program.md)
- [2. Build a small HTTP service](docs/src/tutorials/02-http-service.md)
- [3. Model your data with types](docs/src/tutorials/03-modelling-data.md)
- [4. Make illegal states unrepresentable](docs/src/tutorials/04-refined-types.md)
- [5. Add a stateful agent](docs/src/tutorials/05-stateful-agent.md)
- [6. Test it](docs/src/tutorials/06-testing.md)

### Guides (task-focused)
- [The type system](docs/src/guides/type-system/index.md): refined/sum/opaque types, `match`, `is`, `Result`/`Option`, JSON decode.
- [Program structure](docs/src/guides/program-structure/index.md): how a program is shaped; consuming other contexts.
- [Effects & capabilities](docs/src/guides/effects-and-capabilities/index.md): the capability model, providers, adapters, sync/async sends.
- [Agents & state](docs/src/guides/agents-and-state/index.md): the agent model, state machines, invariants.
- [Entry points](docs/src/guides/entry-points/index.md): HTTP, cron, queue, WebSocket handlers.
- [Actors & access control](docs/src/guides/actors/index.md): public/authenticated routes, webhooks, authorisation.
- [Testing](docs/src/guides/testing/index.md): unit tests, mocks, integration across Workers.
- [Projects, build & deployment](docs/src/guides/projects-build-and-deployment/index.md): layout, run locally, Cloudflare Workers.
- [Editor & tooling](docs/src/guides/editor-and-tooling/index.md): doctor, formatter, editor support.

### Specification (normative)
- [The Bynk Language Specification](docs/src/spec/index.md): §1 scope, §3 lexical grammar, §4 syntactic grammar, §5 static semantics, §6 type system, §7 meaning by translation, §8 compilation model, §9 diagnostics, §10 conformance.

### Examples (complete projects under examples/)
- `hello-world` (minimal multi-file), `todo` (auth + per-user agent), `orders`
  (storage joins/aggregates), `sessions` (TTL cache), `rate-limiter` (pure
  policy + tests), `uptime-monitor` (cron + KV), plus `link-shortener`,
  `feature-flags`, `event-log`, `webhook-relay`.

## Optional

- [Why Bynk exists](docs/src/about/why-bynk-exists.md) and [Bynk compared to TypeScript](docs/src/about/bynk-compared-to-typescript.md): motivation and contrasts.
- [Versioning & roadmap](docs/src/about/versioning-and-roadmap.md): what is shipped vs deferred to v1.
- [Planned features (non-normative)](docs/src/spec/appendix-planned.md): events and sagas are designed but not yet implemented.
- [Troubleshooting](docs/src/troubleshooting/index.md): common diagnostics with fixes.
- [Contributing](docs/src/contributing/index.md): compiler architecture (lex → parse → resolve → check → emit), testing, docs.
